themes.topic: Intel ($INTC) Q2 earnings beat and strong datacenter/foundry growth
themes.summary: Intel reported a strong Q2, exceeding revenue and EPS estimates driven by robust data center growth and improved foundry execution, leading to a significant stock surge and increased investments in equipment, clean room space, and substrates.

mentions.text:
$INTC (Bloomberg) -- (Adds shares.)
Intel shares are soaring 11% in extended trading, after the chipmaker gave a third-quarter forecast that was much stronger than expected. 
•Read more: Intel Forecast Shatters Estimates on Data Center Growth (1)
•Among other notable movers: AMD

There is unprecedented demand for compute.

Very good $INTC Q2 earnings, as expected: 

“Our Q2 results represent our strongest revenue growth in more than fifteen years.”

- Revenues: $16.1B vs. $14.45B expected (beat by 12% and up 25% YoY) 
- EPS: $0.42 vs. $0.22 expected (beat

themes.topic: Google's accelerating capex confirms AI buildout
themes.summary: Google's recent earnings confirm accelerating capital expenditure, reinforcing the ongoing AI buildout and dismissing previous market narratives, with 2026 capex guidance raised to $195B-$205B and further increases expected in 2027.

mentions.text: GOOG’s print simply confirms what the data, checks and ground work have been indicating all along: capex is accelerating. All the narratives invented over the past month to explain the tape were just that -- narratives.

This Google earnings release makes all the fear in semis from the past month look hilariously stupid.

They will all print crazy earnings, from $LITE to $COHR to $SNDK to $NBIS.

- Cloud revenue growth is accelerating
- Gemini 950M MAUs vs. 750M in Q1
- Raise 2026 capex guidance

themes.topic: Nvidia 800V HVDC power architecture production delay
themes.summary: Nvidia's 800V HVDC power architecture adoption is delayed to 1Q27 for production, with Delta Electronics ramping from 2Q27, due to product design validation issues rather than a change in the technology roadmap.

mentions.text:
▶ Nvidia 800V HVDC to Enter Production in 1Q27; Delta Poised for 2Q Ramp-Up

themes.topic: Nvidia-Amkor $1.5B advanced packaging deal in Arizona
themes.summary: Nvidia signed a $1.5 billion agreement with Amkor Technology, including a prepayment, to bolster chip packaging and test facilities in Arizona, aiming to expand US semiconductor operations for AI and diversify the supply chain.

mentions.text:
$AMKR (Bloomberg) -- Nvidia Corp. signed a $1.5 billion agreement with Amkor Technology Inc. to help bolster the company’s chip packaging facilities, part of a broader push to expand semiconductor operations in the US.

themes.topic: Nokia ($NOK) strong Q2 AI/Optical growth and US capacity expansion
themes.summary: Nokia reported strong Q2 AI & Cloud sales growth (105%) and optical growth (20%), with €2.8B in orders, and is expanding its US manufacturing footprint with a new InP facility in Arizona and packaging expansion in Pennsylvania.

mentions.text:
$NOK earnings were much stronger than the stock reaction suggests.

But to be fair, it's doing exactly what it did last earnings so far, just a bit lower in price. And macro is heavy. 

> Optical grew 20%.
> IP grew 16%, finally showing that the design wins are converting.
> AI &

$NOK sales to AI and cloud customers grew 105% year on year in Q2. Order intake reached 2.8 billion euros.

Management said supply remains the main industry constraint and that customers are placing longer term orders to lock capacity. About half of that intake converts to revenu

themes.topic: Rising oil prices and macro inflation/Fed dilemma
themes.summary: Brent crude briefly topped $100, driving inflation expectations and rates higher, with the 10-year yield at 4.71% and the long bond at 5.19%, while low unemployment numbers indicate a hot economy, trapping the Fed between sticky inflation and a strong labor market.

mentions.text:
Brent briefly topped $100 a barrel this morning, up roughly 36% since July 7.

Inflation expectations and rates are rising sharply again. The 10-year is at 4.71%, the highest since January 2025, and the long bond is at 5.19%.

To make matters more complicated, only 187,000 people

themes.topic: MaxLinear ($MXL) Q2 earnings and accelerating 800G/1.6T optical ramp
themes.summary: MaxLinear reported strong Q2 earnings with significant infrastructure growth, an accelerating 800G optical ramp, and 1.6T products in customer hands, leading to raised 2026 optical data-center revenue guidance.

mentions.text:
Another amazing quarter $MXL 

> Q2 revenue hit $168.8M, up 55% YoY and 23% sequentially

> Infrastructure grew 145% YoY and another 35% sequentially

> Adjusted operating margin expanded to 22.3% from 15.9% last quarter

themes.topic: LPKF Laser ($LPK) glass substrate ramp and 2030 TAM increase
themes.summary: LPKF Laser's H1 financials were weak, but the company is focused on a H2 2026 volume ramp for 2027-28 glass core substrate production, tripling its 2030 TAM estimates to $1.7B driven by AI/HPC, with the broader ecosystem corroborating the 2027 timeline.

mentions.text:
Just some thoughts on LPKF Laser / $LPK / $LPKFF earnings:

If you just look at H1 financials, it's pretty ugly:
- Revenue fell 38.3% to €36.5M
- Adjusted EBIT swung to -€10.4M
- FCF at -€11.5M
- Cash fell to €4.0m from €10.0m at end-2025

themes.topic: Intel Foundry CapEx ramp and process node timelines
themes.summary: Intel is significantly increasing tooling investment for its foundry, focusing on Intel 3, 18A, and 18A-P, with 14A risk production planned for H2 2027 and volume production committed for 2028, and expects 2027 CapEx to increase.

themes.topic: Global semiconductor fab construction boom for AI
themes.summary: SEMI Taiwan President Terry Tsao forecasts 89 semiconductor wafer fab construction projects between 2026-2030 and an additional 50 fabs between 2030-2035 to meet global AI demand.

themes.topic: Credo ($CRDO) strong operating model and optical growth
themes.summary: Credo ($CRDO) exhibits a strong operating model with broad exposure across AECs, retimers, and optical solutions, with optics expected to generate over $500M in fiscal 2027, maintaining high gross and operating margins.

themes.topic: AAOI's InP manufacturing capacity and substrate sourcing
themes.summary: The bear case for AAOI centers on its ability to execute InP manufacturing, source substrates, and meet demand, with industry checks suggesting the company is managing its substrate visibility and capacity ramp well without needing to outsource.

themes.topic: CW lasers as a bottleneck in AI optics and Sivers' ($SIVE) positioning
themes.summary: CW lasers are identified as a potential bottleneck in AI optics, with Sivers ($SIVE) positioned for opportunities in higher-power, multi-wavelength, and optical I/O through partnerships like Jabil and Ayar Labs for 1.6T pluggables.

themes.topic: UMC and TSMC expanding Silicon Photonics (SiPh) production
themes.summary: UMC has begun mass production of Silicon Photonics (SiPh) and is expanding to 300mm production, paralleling TSMC's separate 300mm expansion in the same technology.

mentions.text:
SiPh Mass Production

$UMC has commenced mass production of SiPh and is expanding to 300mm production, alongside $TSEM’s separate 300mm expansion.
